message:
feat(demo_showcase): Implement tour mode driver with deterministic timing

Add complete tour mode infrastructure for unattended demos:

- TourAction enum: SetFocus, SetSection, OpenHelp, OpenPalette, CloseOverlay,
  CycleTheme, ShowDebug actions for scripted UI control
- TourStep struct: title, description, duration_ms, action, spotlight fields
- TOUR_SCRIPT constant: 12-step demo covering all major features (sidebar,
  focus, command palette, editor, syntax, themes, unicode, preview, logs,
  performance metrics)
- TourRunner struct: manages step index, timing, pause/resume, auto-advance,
  and exit-on-complete behavior with deterministic frame-based timing
- App integration: start_tour(), stop_tour(), tour_next_step(), tour_prev_step()
  methods with proper borrow management
- tick_tour() integration: auto-advances steps based on elapsed time

Closes bd-3o0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-29z","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: runtime (input loop, resize, frame pacing, rendering)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Runtime + Rendering Pipeline

## Purpose

Implement the reliable “application spine” for the demo:

- Terminal setup / cleanup (alt screen, mouse, cursor)
- Non-blocking input read + `InputParser`
- Event dispatch (keyboard/mouse/paste/focus/resize)
- Resize handling (`renderer.resize`)
- Frame pacing / animations / time base
- Render pass orchestration:
  - background
  - panel layout + scissoring
  - overlays (modals, command palette)
  - debug/perf overlays

## Why This Needs Its Own Epic

The demo’s *wow factor* will collapse if the runtime stutters, flickers, or breaks on resize.
This epic ensures correctness and smoothness first, so later UI beads can focus on content.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Clean startup/shutdown every time (Drop-safe)
- [ ] Input loop is non-blocking and robust to partial sequences
- [ ] Resize updates layout + buffers without panics
- [ ] Frame pacing is smooth (target 60 FPS, reasonable CPU)
- [ ] Rendering is layered consistently (panels + overlays + debug)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:01.984593516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:11.689153365Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","epic","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29z","depends_on_id":"bd-1co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:04:02.001277094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b3","title":"Fix potential integer overflow in buffer size calculations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T16:36:10.348376332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:33.940342789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:38:33.940281014Z","close_reason":"Fixed integer overflow in renderer total_cells calculation using saturating_mul","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2bnv","title":"demo_showcase tests: capability + size degradation matrix (headless snapshots)","description":"# demo_showcase Tests — Capability + Size Degradation Matrix (Headless Snapshots)

## Purpose

`bd-1ob` defines resilience rules and capability gating. We need regression coverage that proves:

- the demo stays usable across constrained terminals
- the *same* constraints continue to work as the demo evolves

This bead adds a matrix of deterministic headless runs that snapshot the demo’s JSON dump across:

- multiple terminal sizes (including "too small" -> compact layout)
- multiple capability presets (no truecolor / no hyperlinks / no mouse / minimal)

## Prerequisites / Contract

This bead assumes headless mode supports:

- `--headless-dump-json`
- `--headless-size <WxH>` (or equivalent) to force a specific buffer size
- `--cap-preset <name>` (or equivalent) to force effective capability flags used by gating

The JSON output must include (at minimum):

- effective capabilities actually used by the demo (after overrides)
- selected layout mode (full vs compact)
- a small set of sentinel markers (panel headers, mode, warnings)

## Matrix (Initial)

Run and snapshot these scenarios (exact names can evolve, but keep the set stable):

1) `ideal_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: ideal/auto

2) `no_truecolor_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_truecolor

3) `no_hyperlinks_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_hyperlinks

4) `no_mouse_120x40`
   - size: 120x40
   - cap preset: no_mouse

5) `minimal_80x24`
   - size: 80x24
   - cap preset: minimal

6) `tiny_50x15`
   - size: 50x15
   - cap preset: minimal
   - expectation: compact layout triggers

## Assertions (Beyond Snapshots)

For each scenario, assert simple invariants in addition to insta snapshots:

- process exits 0
- JSON includes `warnings` when capabilities are missing
- `tiny_50x15` reports compact layout active
- hyperlink markers are absent when cap preset disables hyperlinks

## Logging / Artifacts

On failure:

- print exact reproduction command
- store JSON and stdout/stderr as artifacts when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`

## Why This Matters

Capability regressions are the most common way for demos to feel "broken" on someone else’s machine.
This matrix makes resilience an enforced property.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- Integration tests run the defined headless matrix scenarios (size + cap preset) and exit 0 for each.
- Each scenario’s JSON dump is snapshotted with insta and remains deterministic across runs.
- Tests include extra invariants beyond snapshots (e.g., `tiny_50x15` reports compact layout; OSC 8 absent when hyperlinks disabled).
- On failure, logs include reproduction commands and artifacts are written when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.188432073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:19.403799663Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","resilience","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.213975224Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.217677885Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-2el","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.210212961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2bnv","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:28.206280571Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2c6","title":"demo_showcase UI: logs panel (event stream + hyperlink styling + scroll)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Logs Panel (Events + Hyperlinks)

## Purpose

Logs panel provides:

- “real app” texture
- proof of styled text + colors
- proof of hyperlinks via OSC 8 (`LinkPool`)
- proof of scroll + scissor clipping

## Content

- Deterministic log stream derived from time
- Levels: INFO/WARN/ERROR with distinct styling
- A few special rows that contain a URL and are rendered as a hyperlink

## Hyperlink Strategy

- Allocate URL ids from `renderer.link_pool()`
- Apply `Style::fg(...).with_underline().with_link(link_id)` to the cell range

Note: terminals handle click-to-open; we still render pressed/hover state visually.

## Interaction

- Scroll wheel scrolls logs when hovered
- Click a row to select it
- Selected row shows details in a small “inspector” area (or toast)","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Logs render with level styling and timestamps
- [ ] Panel scrolls and clips correctly
- [ ] At least one OSC 8 hyperlink is rendered
- [ ] Clicking a row selects it (HitGrid)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.206857206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:33:20.483686243Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:56.725549700Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:57.283113630Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:19.154424991Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.233835087Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.195606978Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2c6","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:28.336381372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Note: terminals handle click-to-open; we still render pressed/hover state visually.

- Click a row to select it
- Selected row shows details in a small “inspector” area (or toast)","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:

 {"id":"bd-2el","title":"demo_showcase runtime: headless smoke mode (no TTY, render-to-memory, exit 0)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Headless Smoke Mode (No TTY, Render-to-Memory, Exit 0)

## Purpose

We want a CI-friendly way to validate that the demo binary:

- compiles
- can construct its app state
- can execute its render pipeline

…without requiring a real terminal.

This enables deterministic tests, fast iteration, and regression snapshots.

## Behavior

Primary flag:

- --headless-smoke

When enabled:

- do not enter alt screen
- do not enable raw mode
- do not attempt mouse tracking
- build App + DemoContent
- render into an in-memory OptimizedBuffer
- exercise diff computation between frames (via BufferDiff::compute) to prove the diff path
- exit 0 if all frames render without panic

## Frame Count / Termination Rules

There are two headless execution modes:

### A) Plain headless smoke (no tour)

- default: run a small fixed N frames (fast in CI)
- if `--max-frames <N>` is provided, use that as the frame count

### B) Headless tour run (`--tour`)

- default: run until the tour script completes, then exit 0
  - (implementation can be "fast" by advancing time deterministically without sleeping)
- if `--max-frames <N>` is provided, treat it as a hard safety bound:
  - exit 0 only if tour completed within N frames
  - otherwise exit non-zero with a clear error

This avoids forcing tests to guess the number of frames required to finish a tour.

## Size + Capability Control (For Matrix Testing)

- `--headless-size <WxH>` forces the headless buffer size (default 80x24)
- `--cap-preset <name>` defines the effective capabilities used by gating and rendering decisions

## Tour Integration

- If --tour is set, headless mode executes the tour script deterministically.
- Tour step markers must be included in the JSON dump.

## Output Contract (For Tests)

Headless mode must print a stable, parseable summary line to stdout so integration tests can assert success without guessing:

- HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>

Structured dump (recommended for deeper tests):

- --headless-dump-json

When enabled, print JSON to stdout containing (minimum):

- config summary (theme, fps cap, seed, flags)
- effective capabilities used (after cap preset / gating)
- headless size used (WxH)
- selected layout mode (full vs compact)
- warnings list (e.g., "no_truecolor", "no_mouse", "no_hyperlinks")
- per-frame stats (dirty cell count, dt)
- sentinel markers proving panels rendered (top bar title, panel headers, current section)
- tour step markers (if tour enabled)

Avoid dumping the full buffer unless explicitly requested (too large for CI logs).

## Implementation Notes

This likely requires the demo’s render pipeline to be factored into a pure function:

- fn render_frame(app, layout, buffer, pool, links, now)

Renderer I/O should be a thin wrapper around that.

## Acceptance Notes

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke` works in non-TTY CI environments.
- Results are deterministic across runs.
- Output contains HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK on success.
- JSON schema includes the fields required by `bd-2649` and `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke` works in non-TTY CI environments and exits 0.
- Success output includes `HEADLESS_SMOKE_OK` with stable key/value fields.
- With `--headless-dump-json`, stdout includes valid JSON containing: effective caps, headless size, layout mode, warnings, and sentinel markers.
- `--headless-size` and `--cap-preset` change the effective caps/size reflected in JSON (enabling matrix snapshots).
- With `--tour`, headless execution completes the tour and exits 0 by default; `--max-frames` can bound runtime and fails clearly if too low.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.942661331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:50.268656497Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:05.964184936Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.868058851Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.567529855Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-3lio","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:50.268631831Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:14.001649143Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2el","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:13.424913256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ib","title":"Improve render_to/render_to_with_pool documentation","description":"The render_to() method creates placeholders for multi-codepoint graphemes when no pool is available. This behavior is intentional but not well documented. Tasks: (1) Add doc comments to render_to() warning about placeholder behavior, (2) Add doc comments to render_to_with_pool() explaining when to use it, (3) Consider adding example usage in README. See test test_render_emoji_produces_placeholder and test_render_emoji_with_pool.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:40:47.761765989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:45.133867508Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:45.133807647Z","close_reason":"Documentation already in place - render_to() and render_to_with_pool() both have comprehensive doc comments explaining grapheme handling behavior, when to use each method, and includes a code example.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2iv","title":"demo_showcase runtime: CLI args + Config (modes, toggles, fps cap)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — CLI Args + Config

## Purpose

Provide a minimal, dependency-free CLI surface so the demo is easy to run in different modes (interactive, tour, headless testing) while staying deterministic.

## Constraints

- No new deps for CLI parsing.
- Keep parsing strict and predictable.
- Default behavior must be deterministic (no time-based randomness).

## Proposed Flags

Core interactive flags:

- --help: print usage
- --tour: start tour mode immediately
- --fps <N>: cap FPS (sleep to maintain)
- --no-mouse: don’t enable mouse tracking
- --no-alt-screen: don’t enter alt screen
- --no-cap-queries: skip capability queries

Deterministic termination controls (critical for E2E + recordings):

- --max-frames <N>: hard frame budget; exit 0 after presenting N frames
- --exit-after-tour: when tour reaches the last step, exit 0 automatically

Headless/testing flags:

- --headless-smoke: run a deterministic headless render sequence and exit
- --headless-dump-json: dump structured JSON (for snapshots/regressions)
- --headless-check <name>: run a specific deterministic check and exit (layout/palette/hitgrid…)
- --headless-size <WxH>: force the headless buffer size (default 80x24)
  - parsing: accept `80x24` (lowercase x), reject ambiguous formats

Capability override (for matrix tests + debugging):

- --cap-preset <name>
  - allowed (initial): auto | ideal | no_truecolor | no_hyperlinks | no_mouse | minimal
  - semantics:
    - interactive (TTY): presets can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset)
      - NOTE: `ideal` behaves like `auto` in interactive mode (cannot force-enable terminal features).
    - headless: preset defines the effective capabilities directly

Optional/advanced:

- --seed <N>: deterministic parameterization for animations (optional)
- --threaded: use ThreadedRenderer backend (optional)

## Determinism Rules

- Default seed is a fixed constant (e.g., 0).
- Never default to using time for seed.
- Tour mode must be deterministic regardless of seed (either force seed=0, or make tour steps independent of seed).

## Config Shape

Example:

struct Config {
    start_in_tour: bool,
    fps_cap: Option<u32>,
    enable_mouse: bool,
    use_alt_screen: bool,
    query_capabilities: bool,

    // Deterministic termination
    max_frames: Option<u64>,
    exit_after_tour: bool,

    // Headless/test
    headless_smoke: bool,
    headless_dump_json: bool,
    headless_check: Option<String>,
    headless_size: Option<(u16, u16)>,

    // Capability override
    cap_preset: Option<CapPreset>,

    // Advanced
    threaded: bool,
    seed: u64,
}

impl Config {
    fn renderer_options(&self) -> RendererOptions { ... }
}

## Notes

- Run-loop semantics for `--max-frames` are tracked in `bd-3lio`.
- Run-loop semantics for `--exit-after-tour` are tracked in `bd-3vjm`.
- The primary consumer of `--cap-preset` + `--headless-size` is the degradation matrix suite in `bd-2bnv`.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] `demo_showcase --help` prints usage
- [ ] Flags parsed without external deps
- [ ] `Config` cleanly maps into `RendererOptions`
- [ ] `--headless-smoke` works without a TTY","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.760572467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:27:13.335111682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:27:13.335041732Z","close_reason":"Completed: Implemented full CLI parsing with --help, --fps, --no-mouse, --max-frames, --headless-smoke, --cap-preset and other flags. 10 unit tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:14.034409174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:45.773427812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2iv","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:42.537129324Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2t1r","title":"demo_showcase features: OSC 8 hyperlinks prove-out (LinkPool in logs/help/palette)","description":"# demo_showcase Features — OSC 8 Hyperlinks Prove‑Out (LinkPool)

## Purpose

OpenTUI supports OSC 8 hyperlinks via `LinkPool` + `TextAttributes::link_id`. This bead ensures hyperlinks appear in multiple real UI contexts.

## Requirements

- Allocate URLs through `renderer.link_pool().alloc(url)`.
- Apply link IDs via `Style::with_link(id)`.

Hyperlinks must appear in at least:

- Logs panel: one or more log entries contain a clickable URL.
- Help overlay: “Docs / Links” section is clickable.
- Command palette: a footer row or command description contains a clickable URL.

## Capability Gating

If the terminal does not support hyperlinks (`capabilities.hyperlinks == false`):

- Render the same text but without link attributes.
- Add a subtle `(no hyperlinks)` indicator in the inspector/status bar.

## Acceptance Notes

- In hyperlink-capable terminals, clicking a rendered link opens the URL (terminal-dependent).
- In non-capable terminals, the UI still looks good and makes the limitation explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- Hyperlinks render in all three contexts: Logs panel, Help overlay, and Command palette.
- Link IDs are allocated via `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link(id)`.
- In hyperlink-capable mode, PTY/E2E capture observes at least one OSC 8 hyperlink sequence.
- With hyperlinks disabled (e.g., `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` or detected capabilities), OSC 8 sequences are absent and the UI shows a subtle capability warning/indicator.
- Headless JSON markers include at least one linkified row when hyperlinks are enabled.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:39.954930425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:16:42.664200554Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.051541172Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:23.371289209Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-1w9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:22.792183095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-2c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:21.631300478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-37p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:27:39.971075913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-3pe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:22.215789467Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t1r","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:18.638767113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2t3","title":"demo_showcase runtime: animation clock + frame pacing (dt, easing, fps cap)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Animation Clock + Frame Pacing

## Purpose

Make the demo feel alive and premium:

- smooth animations (header gradient pulse, chart motion, selection transitions)
- stable dt handling
- optional FPS cap

## Time Model

- Use `Instant` to compute `dt` per frame.
- Keep a monotonic `t: f32` seconds for animations.
- Clamp dt to avoid huge jumps (e.g., after resize / backgrounding).

## Easing Helpers (No Dependencies)

Implement a tiny set of easing functions:

- `smoothstep`
- `ease_in_out_cubic`
- `pulse = 0.5 + 0.5 * sin(t * ω)`

## FPS Cap

If `--fps N` is set:

- target frame duration = `1/N`
- after `present()`, sleep the remaining time

Important: don’t sleep negative; also don’t accumulate drift.

## Pause Semantics

If terminal focus is lost:

- set `paused = true`
- stop advancing animation time
- keep rendering (or drop to low FPS) so UI shows “paused”","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] dt/t model exists and is stable
- [ ] Small set of easing helpers exist (no extra deps)
- [ ] Optional FPS cap works

-{"id":"bd-2tj","title":"demo_showcase UI: editor panel (EditBuffer + EditorView + highlighting + wrap/undo)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Editor Panel

## Purpose

The editor panel is the “serious app” anchor:

- proves rope-backed editing (`EditBuffer`)
- proves undo/redo
- proves wrapping + visual navigation
- proves syntax highlighting + theme switching

## Requirements

- Use `EditorView` as the renderer for the panel.
- Load sample Rust content from the content pack (`bd-1ei`).
- Enable highlighting via `TokenizerRegistry::with_builtins()` and `Theme`.

## Interactions

- Standard editor keys from spec (`bd-3l0`): arrows, word nav, home/end, page up/down
- `Ctrl+Z` undo, `Ctrl+Y` redo
- `Ctrl+W` cycle wrap mode
- `Ctrl+L` toggle line numbers
- Bracketed paste:
  - `Event::Paste` inserts into the editor at cursor
  - show toast `Pasted N chars`

## Rendering

- Panel header includes:
  - file name
  - wrap mode
  - theme name
  - dirty indicator (simulated)

## Notes / Known Constraints

- `EditorView` currently renders via `TextBufferView::render_to` (placeholder grapheme IDs).
  - For the editor content, keep multi-codepoint graphemes out of the default sample.
  - Grapheme pool prove-out will occur in a dedicated Unicode panel.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Editor panel renders inside its rect and clips correctly
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Undo/redo works and is visible
- [ ] Wrap modes work (None/Word/Char)

- [ ] Paste inserts content and shows toast","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:37.774610286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:26.302939956Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","text","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:53.387284578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:53.935875752Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:54.500590553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:37.792555333Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.155974038Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2tj","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:26.302913466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2x0","title":"[EPIC] Syntax Highlighting System","description":"# Epic: Syntax Highlighting System

## Background & Motivation

OpenTUI currently has a basic `SyntaxStyleRegistry` in `src/highlight/` that provides infrastructure for registering named styles (keyword, string, comment, etc.) but has NO actual tokenization or highlighting implementation. This is a significant gap because:

1. **Text editors are a primary use case** - The EditorView component exists specifically for building editor-like applications, but without syntax highlighting, it's severely limited
2. **The architecture is ready** - StyledSegment, TextBuffer, and the rendering pipeline already support styled text spans; we just need tokenization
3. **Competitive necessity** - Any serious terminal UI library needs syntax highlighting to be viable for developer tools

## Goals

1. Implement a flexible, extensible tokenizer architecture that can support multiple languages
2. Provide built-in support for common languages (Rust, Python, JavaScript, Markdown, JSON, TOML)
3. Integrate seamlessly with existing TextBuffer and EditorView components
4. Support custom themes with easy color scheme switching
5. Maintain OpenTUI's performance standards (incremental re-highlighting, lazy evaluation)

## Design Principles

- **Correctness over speed** - Highlighting should be accurate; we can optimize later
- **Incremental by default** - Only re-tokenize changed regions
- **Theme-agnostic tokenization** - Tokenizers produce semantic tokens; themes map to colors
- **No external dependencies** - Keep the library lean; avoid tree-sitter or similar heavy deps

## Non-Goals

- Full LSP/semantic highlighting (that's application-level)
- Every language under the sun (users can add custom tokenizers)
- Perfect parsing (we're doing syntax highlighting, not compilation)

## Success Criteria

- Can highlight Rust code in the editor example with keywords, strings, comments colored
- Can switch themes at runtime without re-tokenizing
- Incremental highlighting works (editing middle of file doesn't re-highlight entire file)
- At least 5 built-in language tokenizers
- Comprehensive test coverage for tokenizers

## Estimated Scope

~2000-3000 LOC across tokenizer infrastructure, language implementations, themes, and tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:06.318637099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:23:16.952312825Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:23:16.952293578Z","close_reason":"All subtasks completed: tokenizers, themes, and integration done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","highlight"]}
 {"id":"bd-2x0.1","title":"Define Token and TokenKind types","description":"# Task: Define Token and TokenKind types

## Context

This is the foundational data model for the entire syntax highlighting system. Every tokenizer will produce `Token` instances, and every theme will map `TokenKind` variants to styles.

## What to Implement

Create `src/highlight/token.rs` with:

### TokenKind Enum

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum TokenKind {
    // Keywords
    Keyword,           // fn, let, if, else, for, while, etc.
    KeywordControl,    // if, else, match, loop, return, break
    KeywordType,       // struct, enum, trait, impl, type
    KeywordModifier,   // pub, mut, const, static, async, unsafe
    
    // Literals
    String,            // "hello", 'c'
    StringEscape,      // \n, \t, \x00, etc. inside strings
    Number,            // 42, 3.14, 0xFF, 1_000_000
    Boolean,           // true, false
    
    // Identifiers
    Identifier,        // variable names, function names
    Type,              // type names (PascalCase convention)
    Constant,          // SCREAMING_CASE constants
    Function,          // function/method names
    Macro,             // macro invocations (Rust: foo!)
    
    // Comments
    Comment,           // // single line
    CommentBlock,      // /* block */
    CommentDoc,        // /// or //! doc comments
    
    // Operators and punctuation
    Operator,          // +, -, *, /, =, ==, etc.
    Punctuation,       // (, ), {, }, [, ], ;, :, etc.
    Delimiter,         // < > for generics, | for closures
    
    // Special
    Attribute,         // #[derive(...)]
    Lifetime,          // 'a, 'static
    Label,             // 'label: for labeled loops
    
    // Markup (for markdown, etc.)
    Heading,           // # Heading
    Link,              // [text](url)
    Emphasis,          // *italic*, **bold**
    CodeInline,        // `code`
    CodeBlock,         // ```code```
    
    // Errors
    Error,             // Invalid syntax
    
    // Default
    Text,              // Plain text, whitespace
}
```

### Token Struct

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Token {
    pub kind: TokenKind,
    pub start: usize,    // Byte offset in source
    pub end: usize,      // Byte offset (exclusive)
}

impl Token {
    pub fn new(kind: TokenKind, start: usize, end: usize) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize { self.end - self.start }
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool { self.len() == 0 }
    pub fn range(&self) -> std::ops::Range<usize> { self.start..self.end }
}
```

### TokenSpan (for rendering)

```rust
/// A token with the actual text slice for rendering
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct TokenSpan<'a> {
    pub kind: TokenKind,
    pub text: &'a str,
}
```

## Design Decisions

1. **Byte offsets, not char offsets** - Byte offsets are faster and work directly with Rust strings. The rope can convert to char offsets if needed.

2. **Granular TokenKind** - We have many variants (KeywordControl vs Keyword) to allow themes to be very specific. Themes can always map multiple kinds to the same style.

3. **No style in Token** - Tokens are semantic; styles are applied by themes. This separation allows runtime theme switching without re-tokenizing.

4. **Copy/Clone for TokenKind** - It's a small enum, should be cheap to copy.

## Testing

- Test Token construction and accessors
- Test TokenKind is Copy
- Test TokenSpan lifetime handling
- Property tests for range validity (start <= end)

## Files to Create/Modify

- CREATE: `src/highlight/token.rs`
- MODIFY: `src/highlight/mod.rs` (add `pub mod token; pub use token::*;`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TokenKind covers all common syntax elements
- [ ] Token struct is efficient (no heap allocation)
- [ ] All types implement necessary traits (Debug, Clone, PartialEq)
- [ ] Unit tests pass
- [ ] `cargo clippy` passes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:59.510660429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:17:46.361240373Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:17:46.361221147Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","highlight"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3l0","title":"demo_showcase spec: keybindings + interaction model (keyboard/mouse/focus)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Keybindings + Interaction Model

## Philosophy

- **Discoverable**: status bar + help overlay always tell you what to do.
- **Low cognitive load**: a small set of global chords; panel-local keys only when focused.
- **Mouse is first-class**: click to focus; scroll to scroll; clickable buttons prove HitGrid.

## Global Keybindings (Always Active)

- `Ctrl+Q` — Quit
- `F1` — Help overlay (toggle)
- `Ctrl+P` — Command palette (toggle)
- `Ctrl+T` — Tour mode (toggle / start from step 1)
- `Ctrl+D` — Toggle OpenTUI debug overlay (fps/cells/memory)
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` — Cycle focused panel
- `Ctrl+R` — Force full redraw (`renderer.invalidate()`)

## Panel Focus Model

There is always exactly one **focused panel**:

- Focus is drawn (border highlight + subtle glow)
- Keyboard events route primarily to the focused panel
- Mouse click inside a panel sets focus

Panels:
- Sidebar
- Editor
- Preview
- Logs

## Mouse Interaction Model (Must Exercise HitGrid)

- Click on sidebar items selects section
- Click on top bar “buttons” toggles overlays (Help / Palette / Tour)
- Scroll wheel over sidebar/logs scrolls that panel
- Click on hyperlink-like rows shows visual pressed feedback (even though link open is handled by terminal)

Implementation hint: register hit areas for:

- Each sidebar row
- Each top-bar button
- Selected preview “tabs”

## Input Events to Explicitly Surface

The app should *visibly react* to:

- Focus gained/lost events:
  - When focus lost: pause animations, show “PAUSED (focus lost)” badge
  - When focus gained: resume
- Bracketed paste:
  - Paste into editor inserts text and shows toast “Pasted N chars”
- Resize:
  - Layout recompute + toast “Resized to WxH”

## Editor Keybindings (When Editor Focused)

Keep it close to existing `examples/editor.rs`:

- Arrow keys: move cursor
- `Ctrl+Left/Right`: word navigation
- `Home/End`: line start/end
- `PageUp/PageDown`: scroll
- `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y`: undo/redo
- `Ctrl+W`: cycle wrap mode (None → Word → Char)
- `Ctrl+L`: toggle line numbers

## Overlay Keybindings

- Help overlay:
  - `Esc` / `F1` to close
- Command palette:
  - `Esc` close
  - `Enter` activate selected action
  - Up/Down navigate list
- Tour:
  - `Enter` / `Space` next step
  - `Backspace` previous step
  - `Esc` exit tour","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Global keybindings defined and minimal
- [ ] Focus model defined and consistent
- [ ] Mouse behaviors defined (click, scroll, hit testing targets)
- [ ] Focus gained/lost behavior defined (pause/resume)
- [ ] Bracketed paste behavior defined (insert + toast)
- [ ] Editor keys defined (undo/redo, wrap, line numbers, navigation)
- [ ] Overlay keys defined (help/palette/tour)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:42.145358418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:13.775553150Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:13.775424420Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3l0","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:42.168791836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3l0","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:55.958980961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lio","title":"demo_showcase runtime: hard frame budget (--max-frames)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Hard Frame Budget (`--max-frames`)

## Purpose

We need a deterministic, mode-independent way to bound runtime so we can:

- run PTY E2E tests without hanging
- run quick headless smoke checks with consistent cost
- record short, repeatable demo clips

This bead implements the *runtime semantics* of a hard frame budget.

## Behavior

- Flag: `--max-frames <N>`
- After the app has **presented N frames**, it exits 0.

Counting rules:

- Count a “frame” after a successful `present()` (or after a successful headless render+diff step).
- The counter applies in **all modes**:
  - interactive normal
  - interactive tour
  - headless smoke
  - headless tour

Exit contract:

- Print a stable summary line to stdout:
  - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>`

## Design Notes

- This is intentionally separate from `--exit-after-tour`:
  - `--exit-after-tour` is a tour completion feature.
  - `--max-frames` is a generic safety bound and developer tool.

## Downstream Consumers

- PTY E2E harness (`bd-61g0`) uses `--max-frames` as a hard safety bound.
- Headless smoke/snapshots (`bd-2el`, `bd-1n67`, `bd-2649`, `bd-bqd1`, `bd-2bnv`) use `--max-frames` to keep runs fast and deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `--max-frames <N>` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode.
- `--max-frames <N>` also bounds headless runs consistently (smoke and tour), counting frames deterministically.
- A stable exit summary line is printed (`EXIT_OK reason=max_frames ...`) and includes `last_dirty_cells`.
- PTY E2E tests can use `--max-frames` as a hang-proof safety bound.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.243660372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270704114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270561729Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.259983646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.267009792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.264016443Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3o0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: tour mode driver (script executor + deterministic timing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Tour Mode Driver (Script Executor + Deterministic Timing)

## Purpose

Tour mode is how we get:

- unattended demos (screen recordings)
- deterministic behavior for tests/smoke runs
- a guided narrative that proves features in a clear order

This bead implements the runtime that executes the tour script defined in `bd-1gy`.

## Behaviors

- CLI flag --tour starts tour immediately.
- Tour runs a fixed sequence of steps:
  - changes focus/panels
  - triggers overlays (command palette/help)
  - scrolls lists/logs
  - types/pastes into editor
  - triggers preview animations / errors / recovery

Determinism:

- no randomness
- timing driven by a monotonic clock + fixed durations
- any synthetic inputs are generated by the tour driver

Automatic termination (for recordings/E2E):

- If `--exit-after-tour` is enabled (see `bd-3vjm` / `bd-2iv`), the app should exit 0 when the final tour step completes.
- `--max-frames` acts as a hard safety bound; it should never be required for a normal tour, but it prevents hangs.

## Interactive + Headless

The tour driver must work in BOTH:

- interactive mode (real renderer)
- headless mode (for CI regression tests)

Headless mode should be able to execute the tour and emit JSON markers that tests can snapshot (see `bd-bqd1`).

## Implementation Approach

- Introduce a TourRunner in the demo binary:
  - state: TourState { step_idx, step_started_at, paused, ... }
  - fn tick(now, app, out_actions)

- The tour runner should not directly draw; it only manipulates app state and injects actions.

- When the tour wants to show something, it should set a TourHighlight { rect, message } that the tour overlay bead renders.

## Acceptance Notes

- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour produces a stable “demo story” without user input.
- Tour can be paused/resumed and cleanly exited.
- Tour never leaves the app in a broken focus/input state when it ends.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process terminates cleanly at completion.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour` produces a complete, deterministic feature walkthrough without user input.
- Tour driving works in both interactive mode and headless mode (headless emits stable tour step markers in JSON).
- Tour controls work: next/prev, pause/resume, and exit without leaving focus/input in a broken state.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process exits 0 when the final step completes and the terminal is restored cleanly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.030533207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:37:53.856219400Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.254927712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.042919278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.993258401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.262929423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:17.664069555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3o0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: tour mode driver (script executor + deterministic timing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Tour Mode Driver (Script Executor + Deterministic Timing)

## Purpose

Tour mode is how we get:

- unattended demos (screen recordings)
- deterministic behavior for tests/smoke runs
- a guided narrative that proves features in a clear order

This bead implements the runtime that executes the tour script defined in `bd-1gy`.

## Behaviors

- CLI flag --tour starts tour immediately.
- Tour runs a fixed sequence of steps:
  - changes focus/panels
  - triggers overlays (command palette/help)
  - scrolls lists/logs
  - types/pastes into editor
  - triggers preview animations / errors / recovery

Determinism:

- no randomness
- timing driven by a monotonic clock + fixed durations
- any synthetic inputs are generated by the tour driver

Automatic termination (for recordings/E2E):

- If `--exit-after-tour` is enabled (see `bd-3vjm` / `bd-2iv`), the app should exit 0 when the final tour step completes.
- `--max-frames` acts as a hard safety bound; it should never be required for a normal tour, but it prevents hangs.

## Interactive + Headless

The tour driver must work in BOTH:

- interactive mode (real renderer)
- headless mode (for CI regression tests)

Headless mode should be able to execute the tour and emit JSON markers that tests can snapshot (see `bd-bqd1`).

## Implementation Approach

- Introduce a TourRunner in the demo binary:
  - state: TourState { step_idx, step_started_at, paused, ... }
  - fn tick(now, app, out_actions)

- The tour runner should not directly draw; it only manipulates app state and injects actions.

- When the tour wants to show something, it should set a TourHighlight { rect, message } that the tour overlay bead renders.

## Acceptance Notes

- cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour produces a stable “demo story” without user input.
- Tour can be paused/resumed and cleanly exited.
- Tour never leaves the app in a broken focus/input state when it ends.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process terminates cleanly at completion.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour` produces a complete, deterministic feature walkthrough without user input.
- Tour driving works in both interactive mode and headless mode (headless emits stable tour step markers in JSON).
- Tour controls work: next/prev, pause/resume, and exit without leaving focus/input in a broken state.
- With `--exit-after-tour`, the process exits 0 when the final step completes and the terminal is restored cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.030533207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666987869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666921105Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.254927712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.042919278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.993258401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.262929423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:17.664069555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3oqa","title":"demo_showcase runtime: event/log routing (emit_event + set_log_callback -> logs panel)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Event/Log Routing (emit_event + set_log_callback → Logs Panel)

## Purpose

OpenTUI includes an event/log callback system (`src/event.rs`). The demo should use it so we:

- prove the module works
- get a realistic logs stream without sprinkling UI code with ad-hoc logging

## What To Implement

- At demo startup:
  - call `set_event_callback` and `set_log_callback`
  - route events/logs into the demo’s internal `LogModel` (ring buffer)

- Provide a small helper API in demo-only code:
  - `fn log_info(msg)` / `fn log_warn(msg)` / `fn log_error(msg)`
  - optionally `emit_event("action", json)` for structured actions

## Concurrency Notes

Callbacks are `Send + Sync` and stored behind a `Mutex`. Keep callbacks fast:

- push into a lock-free-ish queue (or just a bounded `VecDeque` behind a `Mutex` owned by the app)
- avoid allocating large strings in hot paths

## Acceptance Notes

- Core app actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize, focus changes) generate log entries.
- Logs panel reflects these entries without frame hitching.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- demo_showcase installs OpenTUI event and log callbacks at startup and routes them into the app’s log model.
- Core user actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize/focus changes) emit visible log entries.
- Callback handlers are lightweight enough to avoid frame hitches.
- The logs panel shows these entries without ad-hoc logging scattered across rendering code.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.888226798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:38.197559204Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.908976801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-2c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.671602532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.124481842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`)

## Purpose

OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode.

This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications.

## Behavior

- CLI flag: `--threaded`
- When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`.
- The UI + state machine should behave the same.

## Design Constraint

`Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app:

- Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper:
  - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }`
  - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()`

## GraphemePool Note (Important)

If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs:

- Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool.
- Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns.
- If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly.

## Acceptance Notes

- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly.
- Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` runs the full app using `ThreadedRenderer` and behaves like the direct backend.
- Input remains responsive and rendering remains smooth.
- Grapheme pool usage is safe in the threaded path (no leaked refs; correct pool-aware clear/resize semantics).
- The app exits cleanly and restores terminal state.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:34.059502793Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3pe","title":"demo_showcase UI: help overlay (F1) + feature legend","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Help Overlay (F1) + Feature Legend

## Purpose

The help overlay makes the demo self-contained and also proves multiple library features:

- overlay rendering + opacity stack (glass modal)
- scissor clipping (scrollable help content)
- hyperlink rendering via OSC 8 (LinkPool)

It also prevents the “what do I press?” problem for first-time viewers.

## Content Requirements

### Keybinds

Include (at minimum):

- `Ctrl+Q` quit
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` focus next/prev panel
- `Ctrl+P` command palette
- `F1` help
- `Ctrl+T` tour start/stop
- editor keys (arrows, word movement, wrap toggle, undo/redo)
- log/preview navigation keys

### Mouse

- click to focus panels
- click to activate buttons
- scroll to scroll lists/logs

### Feature Legend

Make it explicit where each OpenTUI feature is visible in the app:

- RGBA alpha blending (preview overlays / glass modals)
- scissor stack (sidebar/log scroll clipping)
- opacity stack (overlays + tinted UI sections)
- grapheme pool (unicode panel)
- OSC 8 links (logs + help)
- hitgrid (clickable UI)
- pixel buffers (preview panel)
- diff rendering + sync output (perf/inspector + smoothness)

### Clickable Links

Include a small “Docs / Links” section with OSC 8 hyperlinks:

- repo URL
- unicode width reference

The links must be allocated through `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link`.

## Rendering Notes

- Help content should be scrollable when terminal is small.
- Use clear typography hierarchy: title, section headers, monospace keycaps.
- Use hit areas for the “Close” button and for any tab-like subviews.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- F1 toggles a help overlay that clearly explains keybinds, mouse interactions, and where each OpenTUI feature is demonstrated.
- Help content is scrollable and clipped via scissor when space is limited.
- Help overlay includes OSC 8 hyperlinks (when supported) allocated via `renderer.link_pool()`.
- Close controls work via both keyboard and HitGrid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.642163943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:19:27.570230907Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:04.565909821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666002312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.092958035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.656207314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
